3.4 Application of residue theorem to evaluate real definite integrals
4.1 Taylor's series of a complex function — convergence and radius
4.2 Laurent's series expansion in annular regions
4.3 Zeros, singularities, poles — isolated, removable, essential singularities
4.4 Calculation of residues at poles of various orders
5.1 Fourier integral theorem — derivation from Fourier series
5.2 Fourier integral representation — evaluating standard integral results
5.3 Fourier transform — definition, linearity, and basic properties
5.4 Fourier sine and cosine transforms — computation and applications
5.5 Parseval's identity and its applications to evaluate integrals
6.1 Partial differential equations — formation, classification, order and degree
6.2 Separation of variables method — solving PDEs with boundary conditions
6.3 One-dimensional wave equation — derivation and solution for vibrating string
6.4 One-dimensional heat equation — derivation and temperature distribution
6.5 Two-dimensional Laplace equation in Cartesian and polar coordinates
6.6 D'Alembert's solution of the wave equation
6.7 Two-dimensional heat equation and Helmholtz equation
7.1 Z-transform — definition, standard transforms, and basic properties
7.2 Shifting theorems — first and second shifting theorems with applications
7.3 Inverse Z-transform — partial fraction expansion and long division method
7.4 Solution of difference equations using Z-transform
8.1 Curves in space — position vector, tangent vector, unit tangent vector
8.2 Quadric surfaces — ellipsoid, paraboloid, hyperboloid; equations and sketches
